name: pdfAvaluacioClem.pdf
content:
Term report (3rd term)

Data
Name:                Clémence Joachim
Teacher:             Theresa Galler
Level:               Moussy 2024/2025
Absences:            0

Participation/Motivation
Assessment (Participation / Motivation):    Good
Comment:             Clémence shows a delightful level of enthusiasm and motivation during class activities. She actively engages with various tasks, from knocking down towers made of building blocks to enjoying the bubbles and balloons. Her willingness to participate is commendable; she often offers her hand for surprises and shows excitement in feeding the mini Mousys. Clémence's joyful attitude enhances the atmosphere in the classroom and encourages others to engage.

Learning process
Assessment (Learning process):              Good
Comment:              Clémence is making notable progress in her learning journey. She displays curiosity and eagerness to explore different activities, such as engaging with the building blocks and participating in peekaboo. While she is still understanding the concepts of emotions like happy and sad, her interest is evident during related activities. She successfully demonstrated her emerging comprehension through her participation in feeding and caring for the mini Mousys.

Behaviour
Assessment (Behaviour):                    Very good
Comment:              Clémence exhibits positive behaviour in the classroom, showing kindness and a supportive nature towards her peers. She has started to become more comfortable separating from her parents, approaching the teacher independently, which indicates growing confidence. Her ability to help clean up and engage with the toys reflects her responsibility and willingness to contribute to the class activities.

---

Final comment

Clémence has had a joyful and productive year, showcasing significant growth in her comfort and engagement with class activities. Looking ahead, it is encouraged that she continues practicing her English and exploring different books over the summer. This consistent engagement will serve her well as she transitions into the next year, enhancing her language skills and overall confidence. Well done!

name: pdfAvaluacioHortense.pdf
content:
Term report (3rd term)

Data
Name:           Hortense de Lame
Teacher:        Theresa Galler
Level:         Mousy 2024/2025
Absences:      1

Participation/Motivation
Assessment (Participation / Motivation):   Good
Comment:
Hortense displays excellent motivation during activities and demonstrates a keen interest in participating. She actively assists in knocking on boxes and enjoys using shakers, as well as engaging with building blocks and bubbles. Her eagerness to offer her hand for the surprise shows her growing engagement. Despite her limited verbal communication, her mimicry and involvement highlight her enthusiasm for learning and participation in group activities.

Learning process
Assessment (Learning process):   Good
Comment:
Hortense shows a good understanding of instructions and participates in activities with increasing confidence. She engages in stacking and knocking down building blocks, demonstrating her grasp of concepts like size with the animal cards. While still developing her speech, Hortense effectively communicates through mimicry and by following along during interactive play. Her ability to recognise emotions and respond appropriately is a positive sign of her cognitive development.

Behaviour
Assessment (Behaviour):   Good
Comment:
Hortense exhibits friendly and cooperative behaviour during sessions. She enjoys helping to clean up and locates items like Mousy with enthusiasm. Her participation in games like peekaboo reflects her ability to connect with peers and engage in playful interactions. Although she can be a bit fussy at times, her willingness to follow directions and assist with group activities marks her as a cheerful and supportive member of the dynamic.

---

Final comment

Hortense has had a year filled with exploration and learning opportunities. Her enthusiasm for activities, despite occasional fussiness, has been encouraging. She has developed her ability to engage with others and follow instructions, showing promise for further progress. Looking ahead, continuing to use her English and listening to the audio tracks during the summer will greatly support her development. Well done, Hortense, and keep up the good work!

name: pdfAvaluacioBr.pdf
content:
Term report (3rd term)

Data
Name:                 Brieu Lafontaine-Simon
Teacher:              Theresa Galler
Level:                Mousy 2024/2025
Absences:             0

Participation/Motivation
Assessment (Participation / Motivation):  Very good
Comment:              Brieu shows great motivation and enthusiasm during class activities. He eagerly participates in activities such as stacking building blocks and loves interacting with his peers during games like peekaboo. His joyful spirit is infectious as he helps with tasks like cleaning and finding Mousy. Brieu is attentive to the cues in class, such as recognising when it's time for the surprise song and responding appropriately, which highlights his engagement and enjoyment in the learning process.

Learning process
Assessment (Learning process):  Very good
Comment:              Brieu is making commendable progress in his learning. He demonstrates understanding through his actions, such as when he mimics counting and engages with the animal cards to recognise big and small. His ability to follow instructions, like tickling with flowers and offering his hand during the surprise, shows his developing comprehension. Brieu is also exploring concepts of emotions, learning the differences between happy and sad, which further enhances his vocabulary and relational skills.

Behaviour
Assessment (Behaviour):  Very good
Comment:              Brieu displays positive behaviour in class, characterised by his friendly interactions and willingness to help others. He enjoys engaging in group activities, such as looking for Mousy and playing peekaboo, which fosters a playful atmosphere. Brieu's cooperative nature is evident when he assists in clean-up tasks and participates in structured activities. His playful laughter during interactions demonstrates a healthy social development, making him a joy to have in the classroom.

---

Final comment
Brieu has had a productive year filled with learning and joy. His participation has steadily increased, and his willingness to engage with various activities showcases his progress. As he continues to explore new concepts and refine his behaviour, maintaining this momentum is vital. Encouraging him to use his English over the summer will support his ongoing development. Great job this year, Brieu!

name: pdfAvaluacioVa.pdf
content:
Term report (3rd term)

Data
Name:               Valentin Gaye Liéval
Teacher:            Theresa Galler
Level:              Moussy 2024/2025
Absences:           1

Participation/Motivation
Assessment (Participation / Motivation):    Very good
Comment:            
Valentin exhibits a playful and curious energy in class. He enjoys engaging with various activities, particularly in building with blocks and attempting to blow bubbles. His enthusiasm is apparent when he recognizes animal sounds, such as 'meow meow' for cats, and he enjoys interactive games like peekaboo. Although he can be a bit cranky or distracted at times, he consistently tries to participate, showing a willingness to engage in tasks like counting and helping with clean-up.

Learning process
Assessment (Learning process):                Very good
Comment:            
Valentin demonstrates a developing understanding of basic concepts, such as counting and recognising colours during his activities. He actively participates by counting to five and enjoys activities that involve sorting and cleaning up, showing awareness of matching colours. His engagement with bubbles and building blocks highlights his eagerness to explore new challenges. Encouraging him to continue exploring these activities will further enhance his learning and retention of skills.

Behaviour
Assessment (Behaviour):                       Good
Comment:            
Valentin's behaviour reflects a mix of energy and occasional distraction. While he can be a little cranky and struggle with sitting still, he is also kind and affectionate towards his peers, often giving hugs and showing enthusiasm during activities. He engages well with others, such as when helping to feed the mini-mousys or play with blocks. His enjoyment of group activities, combined with gentle reminders to stay focused, will help him further develop his collaborative skills.

---

Final comment
Valentin has shown considerable growth this year, particularly in his engagement with various activities and peers. While he occasionally struggles with focus and managing his energy, his enthusiasm for learning is undeniable. Reinforcing counting and listening to the audio tracks will help him build further confidence and skills in the coming year.
